segments.s172-g121: نمانم is a 1st-person present/future‑oriented negative: 'I will not remain' / 'I will not stay'. که introduces a subordinate clause (here giving the condition/reason) 'that / while'. رستم is the proper name subject of the subordinate clause. برآساید is a 3rd‑person present/subjunctive/jussive verb 'be appeased / find ease' in an older poetic form; the speaker denies remaining while that happens. ایچ is an emphatic negative 'at all' (intensifying the negation).
segments.s172-g122: همی here is an emphatic particle ('indeed' / intensifier) rather than a progressive. کینه را marks the object 'this hatred / grudge' with را as the definite object marker. کرد is the verb 'to do / make' used causatively ('make / set (a grudge)'). باید is the modal 'must / ought', and بسیچ is an adverbial intensifier 'much / greatly'; together they form 'this grudge must be made great.'
segments.s172-g123: همانگه چو forms a temporal clause 'just then, when'. نزد + proper name means 'near / in the presence of Tahmatan' (نزد + object). رسید is the simple past 'arrived / reached' and closes the temporal clause (— 'when he reached near Tahmatan').
segments.s172-g124: خروشید is a past tense verb 'he roared / gave a cry'. چون here introduces the dependent clause with temporal sense 'when / as'. روی او را is the object phrase 'his face' (او = 'him', را = object marker). بدید is the past tense 'saw' — so the clause literally: 'he roared when he saw his face.'
segments.s172-g125: بدو گفت is the common narrative sequence 'he said to him' (بدو = 'to him'). The following clause is reported content. کایدر here functions interrogatively ('where / why here' in dialectal/archaic usage), and به کین آمدیم is a first‑person plural past 'we have come for vengeance' (به + کین 'for vengeance', آمدیم 'we came'). The order places the reported question after the reporting verb.
segments.s172-g126: و‌گر is a conjunctive/contrasting particle 'or / otherwise / but if' introducing the alternative. لب پر از آفرین is an idiomatic noun phrase 'lips full of acclamation/praise' (لب = 'lip', پر = 'full (of)', از آفرین = 'of praise/acclamation'). آمدیم repeats the past 'we came', so the contrast is 'Have we come for vengeance, or have we come with lips full of praise?'.
segments.s172-g127: چو introduces a temporal subordinate clause 'when'. یزدان is the subject 'God' and نیکی دهش is a nominal phrase meaning 'giver/bestower of goodness' or 'bestowed goodness'; here the verb زور داد follows: زور ('strength') is the direct object and داد is the past 'gave' — together 'when God bestowed goodness, He gave strength.' Word order is subject–object–verb typical of Persian poetry.
segments.s172-g128: از اختر is a prepositional phrase 'from the star(s)'. ت + را together make ترا 'to you' (ت is the 2nd‑person suffix separated in the gloss, and را is the object/definite marker). گردش هور is a compound 'the turning/cycle of splendor' (گردش = turning/circuit, هور = splendor/light). داد is again the past 'gave', so the clause reads 'from the stars He gave you the turning of splendor.'
segments.s172-g97: یکی is the indefinite 'one/someone' introducing an agent; بیشه is the direct object 'grove/thicket'; دید is a past tense verb 'saw'. اندرَان is a locative prepositional compound meaning 'therein/in that (place)', followed by the adjective پهن 'wide' modifying دشت 'plain'. Word order: subject (یکی) + object (بیشه) + verb (دید) + locative phrase (اندران پهن دشت).
segments.s172-g98: که is the connective 'and/that' linking this clause to the next. گفتی here is an archaic/formal past-indefinite meaning 'one said' (Ferdowsi uses گفتی where Modern Persian would use گفتند or گفت). برو is the imperative 'go'; بر + گذشت expresses motion 'over/across' (بر = 'over/upon'); نشاید is an impersonal negative modal 'it is not fitting/possible'; گذشت is the verbal noun/past sense 'to pass'/'passage' — together meaning 'it cannot be passed.'
segments.s172-g99: ز بس is a poetic prepositional phrase 'from so much / because of so much' (ز = from; بس = 'much/enough'). بوی 'scent' and رنگ 'color' are coordinated by و; آب روان is a noun phrase with آب 'water' modified postpositively by روان 'flowing' (i.e. 'running water').
segments.s172-g100: همی is the frequentative/adverbial particle indicating ongoing action (here 'became continuously' / 'grew'); نو شد is a compound verb 'became new/fresh' (نو = new, شد = became). از باد literally 'from the wind' indicates the source or agent of the freshness (wind/breeze). گفتی is again the archaic 'one said' or 'a saying' (here introducing the utterance or sound associated with the wind), and the final روان can be read as the content or character of that utterance ('flowing'/'murmuring') — stylistically compact, typical of Ferdowsi.
segments.s172-g101: پس is a temporal/consecutive 'then'. آن ترک 'that Turk' is the subject. خیره is an adjective 'staring' or 'dazed', and زبان برگشاد is an idiom 'opened his tongue' = 'began to speak' (زبان 'tongue/speech', برگشاد 'opened'). Word order places the descriptive adjective before the verbal phrase.
segments.s172-g102: به پیش is a directional/locative phrase 'in the presence of' or 'before'; زواره is the place or name being referenced (object of the memory). همی کرد یاد is a verbal construction where کرد 'did/made' takes یاد 'remembrance' as object: 'he made remembrance' = 'he recalled / remembered'. همی functions as an aspectual particle indicating ongoing or habitual action.
segments.s172-g103: که is the connective 'that/for which'. نخچیرگاه is a compound noun 'hunting-ground' and سیاوش is the personal name in the genitive relationship (the hunting-ground belonging to Siavash). بد این is a brief predicate 'was bad/ill in this (regard)' — classical/poetic word order puts the quality before the demonstrative.
segments.s172-g104: بر + ین (here orthographic split of برین) introduces a locative/relative 'upon/with this'; بود is the past copula 'was'. مهرش 'his affection/love' is the subject of the following prepositional phrase به توران زمین 'toward the land of Turan' (به = to/for, توران = Turan, زمین = land/country). Overall: 'his love was for Turan.'
